content: We can frame ASMR (Agentic search and memory retrieval) as a dynamic, on-demand 'retrieval priming mechanism that can be integrated into online meta-learning memory consolidation to pre-emptively reduce catastrophic forgetting. By pulling only contextually relevant old memories during each new online meta-learning update, ASMR actively rehearses high-utility outdated knowledge without the computational overhead of full memory replay, turning ad-hoc agent memory search into a built-in forgetting mitigation tool.
source_topics: Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
